items.title: Bondscoach van Noord-Ierland gaat dubbelfunctie aan en tekent bij club
items.summary: Michael O'Neill heeft vanaf heden een dubbelfunctie. Hij is door Blackburn Rovers aangesteld als nieuwe manager. Dat gaat hij combineren met het bondscoachschap van Noord-Ierland.

items.title: Slot rueful as Liverpool injury strikes again
items.summary: Arne Slot has admitted the "last thing" Liverpool need this season is another injury after midfielder Wataru Endo was stretchered off against Sunderland.

items.title: Carrick valt direct in de prijzen na droomstart met Manchester United
items.summary: Michael Carrick is direct verkozen tot Manager van de Maand na zijn start bij Manchester United als interim-manager. De zeges op Manchester City en Arsenal in januari waren voor de 44-jarige Engelsman genoeg om er met de prijs vandoor te gaan.

items.title: Premier League superlatives: The 25 best and worst players at different skills
items.summary: Who's the Premier League's fastest player? The slowest? Best passer? The worst? And more. Here are 25 superlatives from different skills.

items.title: Spurs plan to appoint interim, O’Neill appointed at Blackburn, FA Cup and more – live
items.summary: Latest news, previews and press conferences Ten things to look out for | Email David here Brentford striker Igor Thiago has signed a new contract, extending his deal until 2031, with the option for an additional 12 months. The Brazilian was signed from Club Brugge in February 2024, but had to overcome a knee injury last season before hitting form this term, scoring 17 Premier League goals so far. “I love the club and the people in the club,” said Thiago. “It’s a true love, a real love. When the fans support me, and I see them singing my name and singing my song, it gives me more power. It’s been a great season for us. Everybody has been on the same page. I hope we can get something special from it.” Continue reading...

items.title: Sources: Spurs seek interim head coach by Monday
items.summary: Tottenham are confident of making an interim head coach appointment by Monday as they step up their search for Thomas Frank's replacement, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Rice tells Arsenal to ignore noise after PL lead cut
items.summary: Declan Rice has urged Arsenal to "block out the outside noise" but insisted his teammates remain calm after missing the opportunity to move six points clear.

items.title: Barcelona taught 'lesson' by Atlético in Copa rout
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his side were taught a lesson by Atlético Madrid as they suffered a 4-0 loss at the Metropolitano on Thursday in the first leg of the Copa del Rey semifinal between the two teams.

items.title: Northern Ireland boss O'Neill takes on short-term role as Blackburn head coach
items.summary: Blackburn Rovers have announced the appointment of Michael O'Neill as the club's new head coach.

items.title: Eindelijk witte rook uit München: Bayern voorkomt transfervrij vertrek Upamecano
items.summary: Dayot Upamecano heeft zijn aflopende contract bij Bayern München verlengd tot medio 2030. Daarmee is er definitief een einde gekomen aan een soap die bijna een jaar geduurd heeft.

items.title: Spurs to name interim manager before summer appointment
items.summary: Tottenham are confident of hiring an interim manager for the remainder of the season this weekend, before making a long-term appointment this summer.

items.title: O'Neill to juggle Blackburn & Northern Ireland roles
items.summary: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill is appointed Blackburn Rovers boss until the end of the season in a move that will see him juggle both roles.

items.title: RB Leipzig brengt speciaal glow in the dark-shirt uit
items.summary: RB Leipzig heeft een bijzonder tenue op de markt gebracht. Fans van de Duitse club kunnen vanaf volgende week een speciaal glow in the dark-shirt aanschaffen.

items.title: Transfers Feyenoord: Sterling landt in Rotterdam
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij Feyenoord op de voet. De doelwitten van de Rotterdammers, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Toy tigers and Mike Tyson: inside Gary Bowyer’s Burton as they target Cup shock
items.summary: Manager’s unconventional techniques are designed to bring fun as well as results and he has West Ham in his sights “At times the players must think I’m bonkers,” says Gary Bowyer, the Burton Albion manager, volunteering the time he walked into the dressing room with a tennis racket and ball. It is one of the unconventional techniques he has used to convey his message and tap into their psyche. Every week he explores different themes and stories with his squad – be it bullfighting or UFC – and brings them to life through imagery and props, everything from dragons to toy tigers. He has leaned into boxing and particularly Mike Tyson during an FA Cup run that has led them to a fourth-round tie at home to West Ham on Saturday. “The theme for this week is The Ultimate,” he says, referencing Tyson’s 1987 bout with Tony Tucker to become the undisputed heavyweight champion. “We’ve created this idea of climbing into the ring, the pitch, and away you go. We’re fighting West Ham and we’re going to have to take some blows. What do you do if you get knocked on to the canvas? Get back up or lay there and take it?” Continue reading...

items.title: VAR use becoming too ‘microscopic’, warns Uefa’s director for refereeing
items.summary: Roberto Rosetti singles out slow-motion replays ‘We need to speak about this at the end of the season’ Roberto Rosetti, Uefa’s managing director for refereeing, has warned video assistant refereeing is becoming too “microscopic” and fears use of the technology has strayed from its intended purpose. VAR is rarely far from the headlines and has been the subject of further controversy in recent weeks after a series of high-profile incidents in the Premier League. Rosetti made clear that he was not commenting specifically on VAR’s deployment in England but suggested that, across the board, it is guilty of overreach. “We forgot a little bit, everywhere,” he said. “Eight years ago, I came to London and we discussed what VAR stands for. We spoke about clear mistakes, because technology works so well in factual decisions. In objective decisions, it is fantastic. Continue reading...

items.title: NBA clamping down on 'tanking' after fining Jazz and Pacers
items.summary: The NBA have fined Utah Jazz and Indiana Pacers, with the league saying both teams sat healthy players in recent games and noting that compromising its integrity won't be tolerated.
